author: Athanasius of Alexandria
book: On the Incarnation of the Word.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: On the Incarnation of the Word.
Answer to the Greeks. Do they recognise the Logos? If He manifests Himself in the organism of the Universe, why not in one Body? for a human body is a part of the same whole.

ccel.title: NPNF2-04. Athanasius: Select Works and Letters

source.title: NPNF2-04. Athanasius: Select Works and Letters

title: Answer to the Greeks. Do they recognise the Logos? If He manifests Himself in the organism of the Universe, why not in one Body? for a human body is a part of the same whole.

source.title: NPNF2-04. Athanasius: Select Works and Letters

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
On the Incarnation of the Word.
On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: If He manifests Himself in the organism of the Universe, why not in one Body? for a human body is a part of the same whole.

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: But one cannot but be utterly astonished at the Gentiles, who, while they laugh at what is no matter for jesting, are themselves insensible to their own disgrace, which they do not see that they have set up in the shape of stocks and stones.

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: Only, as our argument is not lacking in demonstrative proof, come let us put them also to shame on reasonable grounds,—mainly from what we ourselves also see.

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: For what is there on our side that is absurd, or worthy of derision?

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: Is it merely our saying that the Word has been made manifest in the body?

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: But this even they will join in owning to have happened without any absurdity, if they show themselves friends of truth.

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: If then they deny that there is a Word of God at all, they do so gratuitously, jesting at what they know not.

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: But if they confess that there is a Word of God, and He ruler of the universe, and that in Him the Father has produced the creation, and that by His Providence the whole receives light and life and being, and that He reigns over all, so that from the works of His providence He is known, and through Him the Father,—consider, I pray you, whether they be not unwittingly raising the jest against themselves.

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: The philosophers of the Greeks say that the universe is a great body; and rightly so.

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: For we see it and its parts as objects of our senses.

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: If, then, the Word of God is in the Universe, which is a body, and has united Himself with the whole and with all its parts, what is there surprising or absurd if we say that He has united Himself with man also.

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: For if it were absurd for Him to have been in a body at all, it would be absurd for Him to be united with the whole either, and to be giving light and movement to all things by His providence.

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: For the whole also is a body.

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: But if it beseems Him to unite Himself with the universe, and to be made known in the whole, it must beseem Him also to appear in a human body, and that by Him it should be illumined and work.

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: For mankind is part of the whole as well as the rest.

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: And if it be unseemly for a part to have been adopted as His instrument to teach men of His Godhead, it must be most absurd that He should be made known even by the whole universe.
